title: Policies for information security
description: A set of policies for information security shall be defined, approved by management, published and communicated to relevant personnel and relevant interested parties.
detailed_explanation: This control requires establishing a comprehensive information security policy framework. The top-level policy should set the direction and be supported by topic-specific policies covering areas like access control, asset management, and incident management.
real_world_application: Draft and publish an ISMS policy document approved by the CEO/board. Distribute via intranet and include in onboarding. Review annually or after major changes.
what_it_aids: Establishes management direction and support for information security. Foundation for all other controls.

title: Information security roles and responsibilities
description: Information security roles and responsibilities shall be defined and allocated.
detailed_explanation: All employees and contractors must understand their security responsibilities. This includes defining RACI matrices for security tasks, appointing an information security function, and ensuring role descriptions include security duties.
real_world_application: Update job descriptions to include security clauses. Appoint an ISMS manager. Create a RACI chart for security activities across departments.
what_it_aids: Accountability and clear ownership of security tasks across the organization.

title: Segregation of duties
description: Conflicting duties and conflicting areas of responsibility shall be segregated.
detailed_explanation: No single person should control all aspects of a critical process. For example, the person who requests access should not be the person who approves it. This reduces fraud and error risk.
real_world_application: Ensure developers cannot deploy to production without a separate approval. Finance requires dual signatories. Access provisioning requires manager approval separate from IT execution.
what_it_aids: Reduces risk of unauthorized or unintentional modification, misuse of assets, and fraud.

title: Management responsibilities
description: Management shall require all personnel to apply information security in accordance with the established policies and procedures.
detailed_explanation: Management must actively enforce security policies, not just publish them. This means leading by example, providing resources, and holding teams accountable for compliance.
real_world_application: Include security KPIs in management reviews. Managers sign off on team compliance quarterly. Security is an agenda item in leadership meetings.
what_it_aids: Ensures tone from the top supports the ISMS. Drives cultural adoption of security practices.

title: Contact with authorities
description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with relevant authorities.
detailed_explanation: Maintain relationships with law enforcement, regulatory bodies, data protection authorities, and emergency services. Know who to contact and how during incidents.
real_world_application: Maintain a contact register of CERT teams, police cyber units, data protection authority (e.g., ICO), and sector regulators. Test contact procedures annually.
what_it_aids: Enables rapid incident reporting and compliance with legal notification requirements.

title: Contact with special interest groups
description: The organization shall establish and maintain contact with special interest groups or other specialist security forums and professional associations.
detailed_explanation: Join and participate in ISACs (Information Sharing and Analysis Centers), security forums, vendor security groups, and professional bodies to stay current on threats and best practices.
real_world_application: Subscribe to CERT advisories, join sector ISACs, attend security conferences, participate in threat intelligence sharing communities.
what_it_aids: Provides early warning of threats, access to best practices, and peer benchmarking.

title: Threat intelligence
description: Information relating to information security threats shall be collected and analyzed to produce threat intelligence.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Organizations must proactively gather, analyze, and act on threat intelligence. This includes strategic (trends), tactical (TTPs), and operational (IoCs) intelligence.
real_world_application: Subscribe to threat feeds (MITRE ATT&CK, vendor advisories). Integrate IoCs into SIEM. Produce monthly threat briefings for leadership. Use threat intelligence to prioritize patching.
what_it_aids: Proactive defense posture. Informed risk decisions. Faster incident detection and response.

title: Information security in project management
description: Information security shall be integrated into project management.
detailed_explanation: Security requirements must be identified and addressed from the start of any project, not bolted on at the end. This applies to IT and non-IT projects alike.
real_world_application: Add security checkpoints to the project management methodology. Include security impact assessments in project charters. Security sign-off required at each gate.
what_it_aids: Prevents costly security retrofitting. Ensures new systems are secure by design.

title: Inventory of information and other associated assets
description: An inventory of information and other associated assets, including owners, shall be identified and maintained.
detailed_explanation: Maintain a comprehensive register of all information assets (data, systems, hardware, software, services, people, premises) with assigned owners and classification levels.
real_world_application: Deploy a CMDB or asset management tool. Maintain a data register with classification. Assign data owners for each system/dataset. Review quarterly.
what_it_aids: You cannot protect what you do not know you have. Foundation for risk assessment and access control.

title: Acceptable use of information and other associated assets
description: Rules for the acceptable use of information and other associated assets shall be identified, documented, and implemented.
detailed_explanation: Define what constitutes acceptable and unacceptable use of corporate assets including email, internet, devices, cloud services, and data. Users must acknowledge these rules.
real_world_application: Publish an Acceptable Use Policy. Require annual sign-off from all staff. Cover BYOD, social media, cloud storage, email usage, and software installation rules.
what_it_aids: Sets clear boundaries for user behavior. Provides basis for disciplinary action if violated.

title: Return of assets
description: Personnel and other interested parties shall return all organizational assets in their possession upon change or termination of their employment, contract, or agreement.
detailed_explanation: When someone leaves or changes role, all physical and logical assets must be recovered — laptops, access badges, keys, tokens, data, software licenses, and access rights must be revoked.
real_world_application: Implement an offboarding checklist integrated with HR. IT disables accounts same-day. Facilities collects badges and keys. Manager confirms all assets returned.
what_it_aids: Prevents data leakage and unauthorized access after personnel changes.

title: Classification of information
description: Information shall be classified according to the information security needs of the organization based on confidentiality, integrity, availability, and relevant interested party requirements.
detailed_explanation: Establish a classification scheme (e.g., Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted) with clear definitions. All information should be classified by its owner based on business impact if disclosed, modified, or lost.
real_world_application: Define 3-4 classification levels. Train all staff on classification. Label documents, emails, and systems accordingly. Map handling rules to each level.
what_it_aids: Ensures appropriate protection is applied based on information sensitivity. Prevents over- and under-protection.

title: Labelling of information
description: An appropriate set of procedures for information labelling shall be developed and implemented in accordance with the information classification scheme.
detailed_explanation: Once classified, information must be labelled consistently across all formats — documents, emails, files, databases, physical media, and screens.
real_world_application: Implement document headers/footers with classification labels. Use email classification add-ins. Label physical media with stickers. Apply metadata tags in document management systems.
what_it_aids: Makes classification visible so handlers apply correct protection measures.

title: Information transfer
description: Information transfer rules, procedures, or agreements shall be in place for all types of transfer facilities within the organization and between the organization and other parties.
detailed_explanation: Cover electronic transfer (email, FTP, API, cloud sharing), physical transfer (courier, mail), and verbal transfer. Include encryption requirements, acceptable channels, and transfer agreements with third parties.
real_world_application: Mandate TLS for email. Require encrypted file transfer for confidential data. Establish data sharing agreements with partners. Prohibit USB transfers of sensitive data.
what_it_aids: Protects information in transit from interception, modification, or loss.

title: Access control
description: Rules to control physical and logical access to information and other associated assets shall be established and implemented based on business and information security requirements.
detailed_explanation: Define an access control policy based on least privilege and need-to-know principles. Cover user registration, authentication, authorization, and access review processes.
real_world_application: Implement role-based access control (RBAC). Require access request and approval workflows. Conduct quarterly access reviews. Enforce MFA for privileged and remote access.
what_it_aids: Ensures only authorized individuals access information and systems appropriate to their role.

title: Identity management
description: The full life cycle of identities shall be managed.
detailed_explanation: Manage identities from creation through modification to deactivation. Each identity must be unique, verified, and tied to a real person or approved service account. Prevent identity duplication.
real_world_application: Deploy an IAM solution. Automate provisioning from HR system. Implement unique user IDs. Conduct regular identity reconciliation. Disable orphaned accounts.
what_it_aids: Ensures accountability through unique identification and prevents unauthorized access through orphaned accounts.

title: Authentication information
description: Allocation and management of authentication information shall be controlled by a management process, including advising personnel on appropriate handling of authentication information.
detailed_explanation: Manage passwords, tokens, certificates, and biometric data securely. Include password complexity requirements, rotation policies, secure distribution of initial credentials, and prohibition of sharing.
real_world_application: Implement a password policy (length, complexity, history). Deploy a password manager. Enforce MFA. Use secure channels for initial credential distribution. Train users on password hygiene.
what_it_aids: Strong authentication prevents unauthorized access and impersonation.

title: Access rights
description: Access rights to information and other associated assets shall be provisioned, reviewed, modified, and removed in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on and rules for access control.
detailed_explanation: Formal processes for granting, reviewing, and revoking access. Access reviews must be periodic (at least annually). Changes in role must trigger access review. Privileged access requires additional controls.
real_world_application: Implement access request tickets with manager approval. Quarterly access certification campaigns. Automated deprovisioning on termination. Privileged access reviews monthly.
what_it_aids: Ensures access remains appropriate over time and prevents privilege accumulation.

title: Information security in supplier relationships
description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the use of supplier's products or services.
detailed_explanation: Assess supplier security before engagement. Include security clauses in contracts. Monitor supplier compliance. Cover subcontractors and the entire supply chain.
real_world_application: Conduct vendor security assessments (questionnaires, SOC 2 review, pen test results). Include data protection clauses in contracts. Maintain a vendor risk register. Annual vendor reviews.
what_it_aids: Extends your security perimeter to cover third-party risks which are a leading cause of breaches.

title: Addressing information security within supplier agreements
description: Relevant information security requirements shall be established and agreed with each supplier based on the type of supplier relationship.
detailed_explanation: Contracts must include specific security requirements: data handling obligations, incident notification timelines, audit rights, data return/destruction on termination, liability, and compliance with laws.
real_world_application: Develop standard security schedule for supplier contracts. Include SLAs for incident notification (e.g., 24 hours). Require annual SOC 2 or ISO 27001 attestation. Right to audit clause.
what_it_aids: Legally binds suppliers to security obligations and provides enforcement mechanisms.

title: Managing information security in the ICT supply chain
description: Processes and procedures shall be defined and implemented to manage the information security risks associated with the ICT products and services supply chain.
detailed_explanation: Address risks specific to the technology supply chain — hardware tampering, software supply chain attacks, compromised updates, and counterfeit components.
real_world_application: Verify software integrity via checksums/signatures. Use trusted repositories. Monitor for supply chain advisories (SolarWinds-type events). Evaluate critical vendor dependencies.
what_it_aids: Protects against sophisticated supply chain attacks that bypass perimeter defenses.

title: Monitoring, review, and change management of supplier services
description: The organization shall regularly monitor, review, evaluate, and manage change in supplier information security practices and service delivery.
detailed_explanation: Ongoing monitoring of supplier performance against security requirements. Review SOC reports annually. Track supplier incidents. Manage changes to supplier services that could impact security.
real_world_application: Annual supplier security reviews. Monitor supplier breach notifications. Require advance notice of significant supplier changes. Track supplier SLA compliance.
what_it_aids: Ensures ongoing supplier security posture matches initial assessment and contractual obligations.

title: Information security for use of cloud services
description: Processes for acquisition, use, management, and exit from cloud services shall be established in accordance with the organization's information security requirements.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Specific controls for cloud adoption covering shared responsibility models, data location, encryption, exit strategies, and cloud-specific risks.
real_world_application: Define a cloud security policy. Evaluate CSP certifications (SOC 2, ISO 27017). Implement CASB for shadow IT detection. Plan for cloud exit including data retrieval and deletion confirmation.
what_it_aids: Addresses the unique security challenges of cloud computing including shared responsibility and data sovereignty.

title: Information security incident management planning and preparation
description: The organization shall plan and prepare for managing information security incidents by defining, establishing, and communicating information security incident management processes, roles, and responsibilities.
detailed_explanation: Establish an incident response plan with defined roles, escalation procedures, communication templates, and classification criteria. Test the plan regularly through exercises.
real_world_application: Document an IR plan covering detection, analysis, containment, eradication, recovery, and lessons learned. Define severity levels. Assign IR team roles. Conduct tabletop exercises biannually.
what_it_aids: Enables rapid, coordinated response to security incidents, reducing impact and recovery time.

title: Assessment and decision on information security events
description: The organization shall assess information security events and decide if they are to be categorized as information security incidents.
detailed_explanation: Not every security event is an incident. Establish criteria and processes to triage events, classify their severity, and determine appropriate response. Use consistent taxonomy.
real_world_application: Define event vs. incident criteria. Implement triage procedures in SOC. Use severity classification (P1-P4). Document decision rationale for audit trail.
what_it_aids: Focuses response resources on genuine incidents rather than noise.

title: Response to information security incidents
description: Information security incidents shall be responded to in accordance with the documented procedures.
detailed_explanation: Execute the incident response plan. Contain the threat, eradicate the cause, recover systems, and preserve evidence. Communicate with stakeholders as defined in the plan.
real_world_application: Follow IR playbooks for common scenarios (ransomware, data breach, phishing). Preserve forensic evidence. Communicate with affected parties. Engage external support if needed.
what_it_aids: Structured response minimizes damage, preserves evidence, and meets regulatory notification requirements.

title: Learning from information security incidents
description: Knowledge gained from information security incidents shall be used to strengthen and improve the information security controls.
detailed_explanation: Conduct post-incident reviews (blameless postmortems). Identify root causes and contributing factors. Update controls, procedures, and training based on lessons learned.
real_world_application: Conduct postmortems within 2 weeks of incident closure. Document findings and action items. Track remediation to completion. Share anonymized learnings across the organization.
what_it_aids: Continuous improvement of security posture based on real-world experience.

title: Collection of evidence
description: The organization shall establish and implement procedures for the identification, collection, acquisition, and preservation of evidence related to information security events.
detailed_explanation: Evidence must be collected and handled in a forensically sound manner to support potential legal proceedings or disciplinary action. Chain of custody must be maintained.
real_world_application: Document evidence handling procedures. Train IR team on forensic acquisition. Use write-blockers for disk imaging. Maintain chain of custody logs. Store evidence securely.
what_it_aids: Ensures evidence is admissible in legal proceedings and supports thorough incident investigation.

title: Information security during disruption
description: The organization shall plan how to maintain information security at an appropriate level during disruption.
detailed_explanation: Security controls must be maintained even during business disruptions (disasters, pandemics, major incidents). Reduced operations should not mean reduced security.
real_world_application: Include security requirements in BCP/DR plans. Define minimum security controls during crisis operations. Test security during DR exercises. Address remote work security during disruptions.
what_it_aids: Prevents adversaries from exploiting disruptions when organizations are most vulnerable.

title: ICT readiness for business continuity
description: ICT readiness shall be planned, implemented, maintained, and tested based on business continuity objectives and ICT continuity requirements.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Ensure IT systems can be recovered within required timeframes (RTO/RPO). This covers DR planning, backup strategies, failover capabilities, and testing.
real_world_application: Define RTO/RPO for critical systems. Implement DR site or cloud failover. Test DR plans at least annually. Document recovery procedures. Automate failover where possible.
what_it_aids: Ensures the organization can resume critical IT operations within acceptable timeframes after a disruption.

title: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements
description: Legal, statutory, regulatory, and contractual requirements relevant to information security and the organization's approach to meet these requirements shall be identified, documented, and kept up to date.
detailed_explanation: Maintain a register of all legal and regulatory obligations affecting information security — data protection laws (GDPR, CCPA), sector regulations, contractual commitments, and industry standards.
real_world_application: Create a legal/regulatory requirements register. Assign ownership for each obligation. Review quarterly for changes. Map controls to regulatory requirements. Engage legal counsel for interpretation.
what_it_aids: Ensures legal compliance and reduces risk of penalties, litigation, and reputational damage.

title: Intellectual property rights
description: The organization shall implement appropriate procedures to protect intellectual property rights.
detailed_explanation: Ensure software licensing compliance, protect proprietary information, respect third-party IP, and safeguard the organization's own IP through technical and legal measures.
real_world_application: Maintain a software license register. Conduct license compliance audits. Use only licensed/approved software. Include IP clauses in employment contracts. Monitor for IP leakage.
what_it_aids: Prevents legal liability from license violations and protects the organization's competitive advantage.

title: Protection of records
description: Records shall be protected from loss, destruction, falsification, unauthorized access, and unauthorized release in accordance with legislatory, regulatory, contractual, and business requirements.
detailed_explanation: Implement records management covering retention periods, storage security, integrity protection, and disposal procedures. Cover both electronic and physical records.
real_world_application: Define a records retention schedule. Implement secure archive storage. Use integrity controls (hashing, WORM storage). Secure disposal procedures. Annual retention review.
what_it_aids: Meets legal retention requirements and protects organizational knowledge and evidence.

title: Privacy and protection of personal information (PII)
description: The organization shall identify and meet the requirements regarding the preservation of privacy and protection of PII as applicable.
detailed_explanation: Comply with data protection laws (GDPR, CCPA, etc.). Implement privacy by design, conduct DPIAs, manage consent, handle subject access requests, and appoint a DPO if required.
real_world_application: Conduct data mapping and ROPA. Implement consent management. Handle DSARs within legal timeframes. Conduct DPIAs for new processing. Train all staff on data protection.
what_it_aids: Legal compliance with privacy regulations and protection of individuals' personal data.

title: Independent review of information security
description: The organization's approach to managing information security and its implementation shall be reviewed independently at planned intervals, or when significant changes occur.
detailed_explanation: Commission independent reviews (internal audit or external assessors) of the ISMS to verify it remains suitable, adequate, and effective. Reviews should cover policy compliance, control effectiveness, and process maturity.
real_world_application: Schedule annual independent ISMS reviews. Engage internal audit or external consultants. Review findings in management review. Track remediation of findings to closure.
what_it_aids: Provides objective assurance that the ISMS is working as intended and identifies blind spots.

title: Compliance with policies, rules, and standards for information security
description: Compliance with the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, rules, and standards shall be regularly reviewed.
detailed_explanation: Managers must regularly verify that information processing and procedures within their area of responsibility comply with security policies. This includes technical compliance checks and procedural reviews.
real_world_application: Conduct periodic compliance checks per department. Use automated tools for technical policy compliance (e.g., endpoint compliance, configuration scanning). Report non-compliance to management.
what_it_aids: Identifies gaps between policy and practice before they lead to incidents.

title: Documented operating procedures
description: Operating procedures for information processing facilities shall be documented and made available to personnel who need them.
detailed_explanation: Document standard operating procedures for IT operations including system startup/shutdown, backup, monitoring, error handling, and scheduled tasks. Keep procedures current and accessible.
real_world_application: Create and maintain IT runbooks. Store in an accessible wiki/knowledge base. Version control all procedures. Review and update at least annually or after changes.
what_it_aids: Ensures consistent, secure operations regardless of which staff member is performing the task.

title: Screening
description: Background verification checks on all candidates to become personnel shall be carried out prior to joining the organization and on an ongoing basis taking into account applicable laws, regulations, and ethics.
detailed_explanation: Verify candidate identity, qualifications, employment history, and conduct criminal background checks as appropriate for the role and data access level. Continue screening for sensitive roles.
real_world_application: Conduct DBS/background checks for all new hires. Enhanced checks for roles with access to sensitive data. Verify degrees and certifications. Periodic re-screening for high-risk roles.
what_it_aids: Reduces risk of insider threats by verifying trustworthiness before granting access.

title: Terms and conditions of employment
description: The employment contractual agreements shall state the personnel's and the organization's responsibilities for information security.
detailed_explanation: Employment contracts must include confidentiality obligations, acceptable use acknowledgment, data protection responsibilities, and consequences of non-compliance.
real_world_application: Include security clauses in all employment contracts. Require NDA signing. Specify security responsibilities in role descriptions. Include consequences for policy violations.
what_it_aids: Legally binds employees to security obligations and sets clear expectations.

title: Information security awareness, education, and training
description: Personnel of the organization and relevant interested parties shall receive appropriate information security awareness education and training and regular updates of the organization's information security policy, topic-specific policies, and procedures.
detailed_explanation: Deliver security awareness training to all staff at onboarding and regularly thereafter. Tailor training to roles (developers get secure coding, finance gets fraud awareness). Measure effectiveness.
real_world_application: Annual security awareness training for all staff. Phishing simulations monthly. Role-specific training (secure coding, incident response). Track completion rates. Test knowledge retention.
what_it_aids: People are the first and last line of defense. Training reduces human error, the leading cause of breaches.

title: Disciplinary process
description: A disciplinary process shall be formalized and communicated to take action against personnel and other relevant interested parties who have committed an information security policy violation.
detailed_explanation: Establish a fair, graduated disciplinary process for security policy violations. The process must be documented, communicated, and consistently applied.
real_world_application: Define a graduated discipline framework (warning, written warning, suspension, termination). Document in employee handbook. Ensure consistency of application. Train managers on the process.
what_it_aids: Deters policy violations and ensures fair, consistent consequences when they occur.

title: Responsibilities after termination or change of employment
description: Information security responsibilities and duties that remain valid after termination or change of employment shall be defined, enforced, and communicated to relevant personnel and other interested parties.
detailed_explanation: Some security obligations survive employment — NDAs, IP restrictions, non-compete clauses, and the requirement to return confidential information. These must be clearly communicated during offboarding.
real_world_application: Conduct exit interviews covering ongoing obligations. Remind leavers of NDA terms. Document ongoing restrictions. Include post-employment clauses in contracts.
what_it_aids: Ensures former employees understand and comply with ongoing security obligations.

title: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements
description: Confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements reflecting the organization's needs for the protection of information shall be identified, documented, regularly reviewed, and signed by personnel and other relevant interested parties.
detailed_explanation: NDAs must be signed by employees, contractors, and third parties with access to sensitive information. NDAs should specify what's covered, duration, exceptions, and remedies for breach.
real_world_application: Require NDAs for all employees and contractors at onboarding. Specific NDAs for projects with highly sensitive data. Review NDA templates annually with legal.
what_it_aids: Legal protection for confidential information and deterrent against unauthorized disclosure.

title: Remote working
description: Security measures shall be implemented when personnel are working remotely to protect information accessed, processed, or stored outside the organization's premises.
detailed_explanation: Address the security risks of remote/hybrid working including home network security, device security, physical document handling, screen privacy, and secure communications.
real_world_application: Implement VPN for remote access. Require full-disk encryption on all devices. Provide privacy screens. Define clean desk policy for home offices. MFA for all remote access.
what_it_aids: Extends organizational security controls to remote work environments.

title: Information security event reporting
description: The organization shall provide a mechanism for personnel to report observed or suspected information security events through appropriate channels in a timely manner.
detailed_explanation: All personnel must know how to report security events and feel safe doing so. Provide multiple reporting channels and ensure reports are acted upon promptly.
real_world_application: Provide a security incident reporting email/hotline/portal. Include reporting instructions in awareness training. Ensure anonymous reporting option. Acknowledge all reports within 4 hours.
what_it_aids: Early detection of incidents through human reporting complements technical detection.

title: Physical security perimeters
description: Security perimeters shall be defined and used to protect areas that contain information and other associated assets.
detailed_explanation: Define physical security zones (public, reception, office, restricted, high-security). Implement appropriate barriers — walls, doors, locks, turnstiles, and access controls for each zone.
real_world_application: Define security zones in floor plans. Install access-controlled doors for restricted areas. Data center in highest security zone with mantrap entry. Visitor management for all zones.
what_it_aids: Prevents unauthorized physical access to sensitive areas and assets.

title: Physical entry
description: Secure areas shall be protected by appropriate entry controls and access points.
detailed_explanation: Implement badge access, biometrics, PINs, or guard-controlled entry for secure areas. Maintain access logs. Escort visitors in restricted areas.
real_world_application: Badge readers on all secure doors. Visitor sign-in and escort policy. Access logs retained for 90 days. Data center requires badge + biometric. Regular access list review.
what_it_aids: Controls who can enter secure areas and maintains an audit trail of physical access.

title: Securing offices, rooms, and facilities
description: Physical security for offices, rooms, and facilities shall be designed and implemented.
detailed_explanation: Apply security measures appropriate to the sensitivity of activities performed in each area. Consider sightlines, sound insulation, locked cabinets for documents, and secure disposal facilities.
real_world_application: Lockable offices for sensitive discussions. Sound masking for meeting rooms. Locked filing cabinets for classified documents. Clean desk enforcement. Shredders available.
what_it_aids: Protects information in physical form from observation, theft, or unauthorized access.

title: Physical security monitoring
description: Premises shall be continuously monitored for unauthorized physical access.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Implement surveillance and monitoring systems — CCTV, intrusion detection systems, alarm systems, and security patrols. Monitor 24/7 for critical facilities.
real_world_application: CCTV covering entry points and secure areas. Intrusion detection alarms on data center. 24/7 monitoring service for critical sites. Retain footage for 30-90 days. Regular system testing.
what_it_aids: Detects and deters unauthorized physical access attempts. Provides evidence for investigations.

title: Protecting against physical and environmental threats
description: Protection against physical and environmental threats, such as natural disasters and other intentional or unintentional physical threats to infrastructure shall be designed and implemented.
detailed_explanation: Assess and mitigate risks from fire, flood, earthquake, lightning, extreme temperatures, civil unrest, and other environmental threats. Implement appropriate countermeasures.
real_world_application: Fire suppression systems in data centers. UPS and generators. Flood detection sensors. Lightning protection. Climate control monitoring. Site selection considering natural disaster risk.
what_it_aids: Protects critical infrastructure from environmental damage that could cause prolonged outages.

title: Working in secure areas
description: Security measures for working in secure areas shall be designed and implemented.
detailed_explanation: Define rules for behavior in secure areas — no photography, no unauthorized recording devices, supervision requirements, and limiting knowledge of secure area locations.
real_world_application: No personal devices in data center. Two-person rule for highly sensitive areas. Prohibit photography in secure zones. Limit knowledge of secure area locations to authorized personnel only.
what_it_aids: Prevents information leakage from within the most sensitive physical areas.

title: Clear desk and clear screen
description: Clear desk rules for papers and removable storage media and clear screen rules for information processing facilities shall be defined and appropriately enforced.
detailed_explanation: When unattended, desks should be clear of sensitive documents and media. Screens should lock automatically. Printers should use secure print. Whiteboards should be erased after meetings.
real_world_application: Auto-lock screens after 5 minutes. Implement secure print (badge release). Clear desk checks. Lockable drawers provided. Whiteboard erasure protocol. Periodic compliance spot checks.
what_it_aids: Prevents casual observation and theft of sensitive information from unattended workspaces.

title: Equipment siting and protection
description: Equipment shall be sited and protected to reduce the risks from environmental threats and hazards, and opportunities for unauthorized access.
detailed_explanation: Place critical equipment in appropriate locations — consider power, cooling, water risk, physical access, and cable routing. Protect against theft, damage, and environmental hazards.
real_world_application: Raised floors in data centers. Servers away from external walls and water pipes. Cable management. Lock down portable equipment. Environmental monitoring (temperature, humidity).
what_it_aids: Ensures equipment availability by protecting against environmental and physical threats.

title: Security of assets off-premises
description: Off-site assets shall be protected.
detailed_explanation: Apply security controls to equipment and media taken off-premises — laptops, mobile devices, backup tapes, and documents. Cover transit security and remote working environments.
real_world_application: Full-disk encryption on all portable devices. Remote wipe capability for mobile devices. Secure courier for media transport. Insurance for portable equipment. Asset tracking.
what_it_aids: Extends physical security to assets beyond organizational premises where they are most vulnerable.

title: Storage media
description: Storage media shall be managed through their life cycle of acquisition, use, transportation, and disposal in accordance with the organization's classification scheme and handling requirements.
detailed_explanation: Control removable media (USB, external drives, tapes) throughout their lifecycle. Include encryption, tracking, secure storage, and certified destruction.
real_world_application: Encrypt all removable media. Register and track removable storage. Disable USB ports where not needed. Certified destruction (NIST 800-88) for decommissioned media. Media handling procedures by classification.
what_it_aids: Prevents data loss through removable media theft, loss, or improper disposal.

title: Supporting utilities
description: Information processing facilities shall be protected from power failures and other disruptions caused by failures in supporting utilities.
detailed_explanation: Ensure reliable power, telecommunications, water, gas, sewage, heating/ventilation, and air conditioning for critical facilities. Include redundancy and monitoring.
real_world_application: UPS for all critical systems. Diesel generators with 72-hour fuel supply. Dual power feeds for data center. HVAC redundancy. Regular testing of backup power systems.
what_it_aids: Maintains system availability during utility failures.

title: Cabling security
description: Cables carrying power or data or supporting information services shall be protected from interception, interference, and damage.
detailed_explanation: Protect network and power cabling from unauthorized access, damage, and electromagnetic interference. Use conduits, avoid routing through public areas, and label cables.
real_world_application: Use armored conduit for critical cables. Separate power and data cables. Fiber optic for sensitive links. Locked patch panels and cabinets. Cable management and labeling. Regular cable audits.
what_it_aids: Prevents wiretapping, cable damage, and service disruption.

title: Equipment maintenance
description: Equipment shall be correctly maintained to ensure availability, integrity, and confidentiality of information.
detailed_explanation: Maintain equipment per manufacturer specifications. Only authorized personnel should perform maintenance. Clear sensitive data before sending for repair. Log all maintenance activities.
real_world_application: Preventive maintenance schedule for all critical equipment. Authorized service providers only. Wipe/remove disks before external repair. Maintenance log for all work. Supervision of on-site maintenance by third parties.
what_it_aids: Prevents system failures and data exposure during maintenance activities.

title: Secure disposal or re-use of equipment
description: Items of equipment containing storage media shall be verified to ensure that any sensitive data and licensed software has been removed or securely overwritten prior to disposal or re-use.
detailed_explanation: Before disposing or reusing equipment, ensure all data is irrecoverably destroyed. Methods include degaussing, physical destruction, or cryptographic erasure. Maintain disposal records.
real_world_application: Implement NIST 800-88 compliant destruction procedures. Certificate of destruction from disposal vendors. Physical shredding for highly classified media. Audit disposal vendor compliance.
what_it_aids: Prevents data recovery from disposed or repurposed equipment.

title: User endpoint devices
description: Information stored on, processed by, or accessible via user endpoint devices shall be protected.
detailed_explanation: Secure laptops, desktops, tablets, and phones with encryption, malware protection, configuration management, and remote wipe. Cover both corporate and BYOD devices.
real_world_application: Full-disk encryption (BitLocker/FileVault). EDR on all endpoints. MDM for mobile devices. Automated patching. Configuration baselines. Remote wipe capability. BYOD policy with enrollment requirements.
what_it_aids: Protects organizational data on the devices where users interact with it most.

title: Privileged access rights
description: The allocation and use of privileged access rights shall be restricted and managed.
detailed_explanation: Privileged accounts (admin, root, service) require additional controls — separate accounts, MFA, session recording, just-in-time access, and regular review.
real_world_application: Separate admin accounts from daily-use accounts. Implement PAM solution. MFA for all privileged access. Session recording for sensitive systems. Quarterly privileged access review. Just-in-time elevation.
what_it_aids: Privileged accounts are the highest-value targets for attackers. Tight controls reduce blast radius.

title: Information access restriction
description: Access to information and other associated assets shall be restricted in accordance with the established topic-specific policy on access control.
detailed_explanation: Implement technical controls to enforce access policies — file permissions, database access controls, application-level authorization, and API access controls.
real_world_application: Implement RBAC in applications. Database access through application layer only. File share permissions based on AD groups. API authentication and authorization. Data masking for non-production environments.
what_it_aids: Technical enforcement of the principle of least privilege at the data and application level.

title: Access to source code
description: Read and write access to source code, development tools, and software libraries shall be appropriately managed.
detailed_explanation: Source code is a critical asset. Control access to repositories, limit who can read/write/merge code, and protect against unauthorized modification or theft.
real_world_application: Role-based access to Git repositories. Branch protection rules. Code review requirements for merges. Audit logging of repository access. Separate access for production deployment pipelines.
what_it_aids: Protects intellectual property and prevents introduction of malicious code.

title: Secure authentication
description: Secure authentication technologies and procedures shall be established and implemented based on information access restrictions and the topic-specific policy on access control.
detailed_explanation: Implement strong authentication mechanisms — MFA, certificate-based auth, passwordless options, and SSO. Protect against brute force, credential stuffing, and session hijacking.
real_world_application: MFA for all users (minimum for remote and privileged access). Account lockout after failed attempts. Session timeout. SSO with SAML/OIDC. Passwordless options (FIDO2/WebAuthn).
what_it_aids: Strong authentication is the primary defense against unauthorized access using stolen credentials.

title: Capacity management
description: The use of resources shall be monitored and adjusted in line with current and expected capacity requirements.
detailed_explanation: Monitor system resources (CPU, memory, storage, bandwidth) and plan for growth. Capacity shortfalls can impact availability. Include capacity planning in change management.
real_world_application: Implement monitoring dashboards for all critical systems. Set alerts for capacity thresholds (80%). Quarterly capacity review and forecasting. Auto-scaling for cloud resources where appropriate.
what_it_aids: Prevents outages and performance degradation caused by resource exhaustion.

title: Protection against malware
description: Protection against malware shall be implemented and supported by appropriate user awareness.
detailed_explanation: Deploy anti-malware solutions across all endpoints, servers, and email gateways. Combine signature-based, behavioral, and heuristic detection. Keep definitions current. Educate users.
real_world_application: EDR on all endpoints and servers. Email gateway filtering with sandboxing. Web content filtering. Automated definition updates. User training on recognizing malicious content. Regular malware scanning.
what_it_aids: Detects and prevents malware infections which remain a primary attack vector.

title: Management of technical vulnerabilities
description: Information about technical vulnerabilities of information systems in use shall be obtained and the organization's exposure to such vulnerabilities shall be evaluated and appropriate measures taken.
detailed_explanation: Implement a vulnerability management program — regular scanning, risk-based prioritization, timely patching, and tracking remediation. Cover infrastructure, applications, and third-party components.
real_world_application: Automated vulnerability scanning (weekly for critical, monthly for all). Risk-based patching SLAs (critical: 72 hours, high: 2 weeks). Patch management process. Vulnerability tracking dashboard.
what_it_aids: Reduces the attack surface by identifying and remediating known vulnerabilities before exploitation.

title: Configuration management
description: Configurations, including security configurations, of hardware, software, services, and networks shall be established, documented, implemented, monitored, and reviewed.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Establish and maintain secure configuration baselines for all systems. Monitor for configuration drift. Include hardening standards based on CIS benchmarks or similar.
real_world_application: Define secure baselines using CIS benchmarks. Implement configuration management tools (Ansible, Puppet, Chef). Monitor for drift. Golden images for server/workstation builds. Regular configuration audits.
what_it_aids: Misconfiguration is a leading cause of breaches. Consistent, hardened configurations reduce attack surface.

title: Information deletion
description: Information stored in information systems, devices, or in any other storage media shall be deleted when no longer required.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Implement systematic data deletion in accordance with retention policies. Cover databases, file systems, backups, cloud storage, and development/test environments.
real_world_application: Automated retention enforcement in email and file systems. Scheduled purge of expired data. Deletion verification procedures. Include backups in deletion scope. Data discovery for rogue copies.
what_it_aids: Reduces data breach exposure by eliminating unnecessary data. Supports privacy compliance (right to erasure).

title: Data masking
description: Data masking shall be used in accordance with the organization's topic-specific policy on access control and other related topic-specific policies, and business requirements, taking applicable legislation into consideration.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Apply data masking, pseudonymization, or anonymization techniques to protect sensitive data in non-production environments, reports, and where full data access is not required.
real_world_application: Mask PII in test/dev environments. Pseudonymize data for analytics. Dynamic data masking in databases for restricted users. Tokenize payment card data. Anonymize data for training purposes.
what_it_aids: Protects sensitive data while maintaining utility for testing, development, and analytics.

title: Data leakage prevention
description: Data leakage prevention measures shall be applied to systems, networks, and any other devices that process, store, or transmit sensitive information.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Implement DLP solutions to detect and prevent unauthorized transmission of sensitive data via email, web, USB, cloud, and other channels.
real_world_application: Deploy DLP for email (block/quarantine sensitive data). Endpoint DLP for USB/print. Cloud DLP for SaaS applications. Network DLP at egress points. Content inspection for classified data patterns.
what_it_aids: Prevents exfiltration of sensitive data whether intentional (insider threat) or accidental (user error).

title: Information backup
description: Backup copies of information, software, and systems shall be maintained and regularly tested in accordance with the agreed topic-specific policy on backup.
detailed_explanation: Implement backup strategy covering frequency, retention, encryption, offsite storage, and regular restore testing. Cover all critical data and configurations.
real_world_application: Daily incremental, weekly full backups. Offsite/cloud replication. Encrypted backup media. Monthly restore tests of random datasets. Backup monitoring and alerting. 3-2-1 backup strategy.
what_it_aids: Enables recovery from data loss, corruption, ransomware, and disasters.

title: Redundancy of information processing facilities
description: Information processing facilities shall be implemented with redundancy sufficient to meet availability requirements.
detailed_explanation: Implement redundancy for critical systems — clustering, load balancing, failover, and geographic distribution. Level of redundancy should match business availability requirements.
real_world_application: Active-passive or active-active clustering for critical systems. Geographic redundancy for DR. Load balancers for web services. Redundant network paths. Regular failover testing.
what_it_aids: Ensures system availability despite hardware failures or site-level outages.

title: Logging
description: Logs that record activities, exceptions, faults, and other relevant events shall be produced, stored, protected, and analyzed.
detailed_explanation: Implement comprehensive logging across all systems — authentication events, access to sensitive data, administrative actions, system events, and security events. Centralize and protect logs.
real_world_application: Centralized log management (SIEM). Log authentication success/failure, admin actions, data access, system events. Protect log integrity (write-once, separate storage). Retain logs per policy (minimum 12 months). Regular log review.
what_it_aids: Provides audit trail for investigations, compliance evidence, and input to security monitoring.

title: Monitoring activities
description: Networks, systems, and applications shall be monitored for anomalous behavior and appropriate actions taken to evaluate potential information security incidents.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Implement continuous monitoring to detect anomalies, suspicious behavior, and potential security incidents. Use SIEM, NDR, and behavioral analytics.
real_world_application: SIEM with correlation rules and alerting. NDR for network anomaly detection. User behavior analytics (UBA/UEBA). 24/7 monitoring for critical systems. Automated alerting with defined response procedures.
what_it_aids: Enables detection of attacks and anomalies that bypass preventive controls.

title: Clock synchronization
description: The clocks of information processing systems used by the organization shall be synchronized to approved time sources.
detailed_explanation: All systems must use synchronized time from authoritative sources (NTP). Accurate timestamps are critical for log correlation, forensics, and audit trails.
real_world_application: Configure all systems to use internal NTP servers synchronized to authoritative sources (e.g., GPS, national time services). Monitor NTP synchronization. Use UTC for log timestamps.
what_it_aids: Enables accurate log correlation across systems. Essential for incident investigation and legal evidence.

title: Use of privileged utility programs
description: The use of utility programs that might be capable of overriding system and application controls shall be restricted and tightly controlled.
detailed_explanation: System utilities (registry editors, disk tools, debuggers, network sniffers) can bypass security controls. Restrict their availability and log their use.
real_world_application: Remove unnecessary utilities from production systems. Restrict access to remaining utilities to authorized administrators. Log all utility usage. Include in privileged access management. Periodic review of installed utilities.
what_it_aids: Prevents misuse of powerful tools that could bypass access controls or modify system configurations.

title: Installation of software on operational systems
description: Procedures and measures shall be implemented to securely manage software installation on operational systems.
detailed_explanation: Control what software can be installed on production and operational systems. Use approved software lists, restrict installation rights, and verify software integrity before installation.
real_world_application: Application whitelisting on critical systems. Restrict local admin rights (users cannot install software). Software request and approval process. Verify integrity of all software before deployment. Regular installed software audits.
what_it_aids: Prevents unauthorized or malicious software from entering the production environment.

title: Networks security
description: Networks and network devices shall be managed and controlled to protect information in systems and applications.
detailed_explanation: Implement network security controls including firewalls, segmentation, monitoring, and secure management of network devices. Apply defense-in-depth principles.
real_world_application: Next-gen firewalls at perimeter and between segments. Network segmentation with defined trust zones. Secure management plane (out-of-band). Network device hardening. Regular firewall rule reviews. Network access control (NAC).
what_it_aids: Provides layers of defense to protect internal systems and data from network-based attacks.

title: Security of network services
description: Security mechanisms, service levels, and service requirements of network services shall be identified, implemented, and monitored.
detailed_explanation: Network services (DNS, DHCP, load balancers, CDN, ISP) must have defined security requirements and SLAs. Covers both internal services and those from external providers.
real_world_application: Security requirements in ISP/network service contracts. SLAs for network service availability. Monitoring of network service performance and security. Redundant DNS and internet connectivity.
what_it_aids: Ensures network services that systems depend on are secure and reliable.

title: Segregation of networks
description: Groups of information services, users, and information systems shall be segregated in the organization's networks.
detailed_explanation: Segment networks based on trust levels and business requirements — separate production from development, isolate sensitive systems, and segment by function and data classification.
real_world_application: VLAN segmentation by function (production, development, management, DMZ). Microsegmentation for critical assets. PCI DSS CDE isolation. Guest WiFi on separate network. Zero-trust network architecture progression.
what_it_aids: Limits lateral movement by attackers and contains breaches to affected segments.

title: Web filtering
description: Access to external websites shall be managed to reduce exposure to malicious content.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Implement web content filtering to block access to known malicious, phishing, and inappropriate websites. Cover all user access including remote workers.
real_world_application: Deploy web proxy or DNS-level filtering. Block known malicious categories. SSL inspection for visibility. Cloud-based filtering for remote workers. Exception request process. Regular policy review.
what_it_aids: Prevents drive-by downloads, phishing, and access to malicious content.

title: Use of cryptography
description: Rules for the effective use of cryptography, including cryptographic key management, shall be defined and implemented.
detailed_explanation: Define a cryptography policy covering approved algorithms, key lengths, key management lifecycle, and use cases. Cover data at rest, in transit, and in use.
real_world_application: TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit. AES-256 for data at rest. HSM for key management. Certificate lifecycle management. Approved cryptographic algorithm list. Annual crypto review for deprecated algorithms.
what_it_aids: Protects data confidentiality and integrity through mathematical assurance.

title: Secure development life cycle
description: Rules for the secure development of software and systems shall be established and applied.
detailed_explanation: Integrate security into every phase of the SDLC — requirements, design, coding, testing, deployment, and maintenance. Include threat modeling, secure coding standards, and security testing.
real_world_application: Security requirements in user stories. Threat modeling in design phase. SAST in CI/CD pipeline. Peer code review with security checklist. DAST in staging. Security sign-off before production release.
what_it_aids: Prevents security vulnerabilities from being introduced during development, when they are cheapest to fix.

title: Application security requirements
description: Information security requirements shall be identified, specified, and approved when developing or acquiring applications.
detailed_explanation: Define security requirements for applications covering authentication, authorization, input validation, cryptography, error handling, logging, and data protection. Include in RFPs for acquired software.
real_world_application: Security requirements template for all new projects. OWASP ASVS as baseline. Security requirements in vendor RFPs. Security acceptance criteria for deliverables. Requirements traceability to tests.
what_it_aids: Ensures security is built into applications from the requirements phase rather than bolted on later.

title: Secure system architecture and engineering principles
description: Principles for engineering secure systems shall be established, documented, maintained, and applied to any information system development activities.
detailed_explanation: Document and apply secure architecture principles — defense in depth, least privilege, fail-safe defaults, economy of mechanism, complete mediation, and zero trust.
real_world_application: Documented architecture security principles. Security architecture review for all new systems. Reference architectures for common patterns. Zero-trust architecture roadmap. Architecture decision records (ADRs) for security decisions.
what_it_aids: Ensures systems are designed with security as a foundational principle rather than an afterthought.

title: Secure coding
description: Secure coding principles shall be applied to software development.
detailed_explanation: NEW in 2022. Developers must follow secure coding standards — input validation, output encoding, parameterized queries, proper error handling, and avoidance of known dangerous functions.
real_world_application: Adopt OWASP Secure Coding Practices. Language-specific coding standards. SAST tools integrated into IDE and CI/CD. Secure coding training for all developers. Code review checklists including security items.
what_it_aids: Prevents common vulnerabilities (SQLi, XSS, buffer overflows) at the code level.

title: Security testing in development and acceptance
description: Security testing processes shall be defined and implemented in the development life cycle.
detailed_explanation: Include security testing at multiple stages — SAST, DAST, IAST, SCA, penetration testing, and security acceptance testing before production deployment.
real_world_application: SAST in CI/CD pipeline. DAST in staging environment. SCA for third-party libraries. Annual penetration testing by independent firm. Security acceptance criteria for go-live. Bug bounty program consideration.
what_it_aids: Identifies security vulnerabilities before they reach production where exploitation risk is highest.

title: Outsourced development
description: The organization shall direct, monitor, and review the activities related to outsourced system development.
detailed_explanation: When development is outsourced, maintain security oversight — include security requirements in contracts, review code quality, retain IP rights, and monitor compliance with secure development practices.
real_world_application: Security requirements in outsourcing contracts. Code quality and security reviews of delivered code. Escrow arrangements for source code. Security testing of outsourced deliverables. Regular vendor assessment.
what_it_aids: Ensures outsourced code meets the same security standards as internally developed code.

title: Separation of development, test, and production environments
description: Development, testing, and production environments shall be separated and secured.
detailed_explanation: Maintain distinct environments with independent access controls, data, and configurations. Prevent unauthorized access to production from development environments. Don't use production data in test/dev.
real_world_application: Separate infrastructure for dev/test/prod. Different credentials per environment. No production data in dev/test (use masked data). Restricted production access. Deployment only through approved CI/CD pipeline.
what_it_aids: Prevents untested changes and unauthorized access from impacting production systems and data.

title: Change management
description: Changes to information processing facilities and information systems shall be subject to change management procedures.
detailed_explanation: Implement formal change management — request, assess risk, approve, test, deploy, and review. Emergency change procedures for urgent fixes with retrospective approval.
real_world_application: Change Advisory Board (CAB) for significant changes. Change request tickets with risk assessment. Testing requirements before production deployment. Rollback plans. Post-implementation review. Emergency change process with 24-hour retrospective approval.
what_it_aids: Prevents outages and security issues caused by uncontrolled changes to systems and infrastructure.

title: Test information
description: Test information shall be appropriately selected, protected, and managed.
detailed_explanation: Protect test data appropriately. Avoid using production data in test environments. Where production data is necessary, mask or anonymize sensitive fields. Control access to test data.
real_world_application: Synthetic test data generation tools. Data masking for production-derived test data. Access controls on test environments. Secure disposal of test data after use. Prohibition on using live PII in test.
what_it_aids: Prevents exposure of sensitive data through less-secured test environments.

title: Protection of information systems during audit testing
description: Audit tests and other assurance activities involving assessment of operational systems shall be planned and agreed between the tester and appropriate management.
detailed_explanation: Plan audit activities to minimize disruption to operations. Restrict auditor access to read-only where possible. Protect audit tools and results. Schedule testing during low-impact windows.
real_world_application: Formal scope agreement for all audits. Read-only access for auditors. Schedule invasive testing in maintenance windows. Protect audit reports as confidential. Secure destruction of audit data copies after use.
what_it_aids: Prevents audit activities from inadvertently disrupting operations or exposing sensitive data.
